compiled_state.title: record-test-status.sh: stale RED marker causes false 'failed' status when test goes GREEN
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
When a test listed in .test-index with a RED marker (e.g., source.sh:[test_name]) starts passing, record-test-status.sh detects the marker and writes status=failed with empty failed_tests. This causes the test gate to block commits even though no tests are actually failing.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Add a RED marker [test_name] to a .test-index entry
2. Fix the underlying code so the test passes
3. Run record-test-status.sh — it writes status=failed due to the stale marker

## Expected Behavior
When a RED-marked test passes, record-test-status.sh should either: (a) warn that the marker is stale and needs removal, or (b) treat a passing RED-marked test as a GREEN indicator requiring human review rather than silently failing.

## Actual Behavior
Writes status=failed with empty failed_tests, causing pre-commit test gate to block with a confusing error (test gate shows failed but no specific test is named).

## Workaround
Remove the [test_name] marker from .test-index, then re-run record-test-status.sh with --restart flag.
